disclaimer: Reference-only support. Confirm urgent, paediatric, transfusion, and site-specific requests with local protocol or senior review.

presentations.label: Chest pain / myocardial injury support
presentations.summary: Useful when ACS or myocardial injury is part of the differential.

tests.name: Cardiac Profile
tests.reason: Current catalogue cardiac markers for suspected myocardial injury.

presentations.caution: Serial timing, ECG findings, and urgent chest-pain pathways still take priority.

presentations.label: Shortness of breath / heart failure support
presentations.summary: Useful when dyspnoea, orthopnoea, or fluid overload raises heart-failure concern.

tests.name: NT-proBNP
tests.reason: Natriuretic peptide support for suspected heart failure.

tests.name: Cardiac Profile
tests.reason: Cardiac marker support when myocardial strain or injury is part of acute decompensation.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: Baseline chemistry support for fluid balance and renal context.

presentations.caution: Interpret with age, renal function, and the local heart-failure pathway.

presentations.label: Respiratory distress / acid-base support
presentations.summary: Useful when oxygenation, ventilation, or perfusion is unstable.

tests.name: Blood Gases
tests.reason: Urgent gas and acid-base support from the current catalogue.

tests.name: Lactate
tests.reason: Perfusion marker often paired with severe respiratory or shock presentations.

presentations.caution: Escalate clinically first if the patient is unstable.

presentations.label: Psychosis / substance screen support
presentations.summary: Useful when psychosis, agitation, or behavioural disturbance raises substance-use concern.

tests.name: Drugs of Abuse Screen (Urine)
tests.reason: Toxicology support when substance exposure is part of the psychosis differential.

presentations.caution: Use the local mental-health, intoxication, and emergency pathway when agitation, violence, overdose, or reduced consciousness is present.

presentations.label: Fever / sepsis support
presentations.summary: Useful when fever, rigors, hypotension, or sepsis is suspected.

tests.name: Blood Culture
tests.reason: Culture support before antibiotics where clinically appropriate.

tests.name: Lactate
tests.reason: Perfusion marker commonly paired with suspected sepsis.

tests.name: CRP
tests.reason: Inflammatory marker from the existing catalogue.

tests.name: Procalcitonin (PCT)
tests.reason: Additional sepsis support where local protocol uses PCT.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: Baseline blood count support for infection severity and cell lines.

presentations.caution: Obtain cultures before antibiotics where possible and follow urgent sepsis protocol.

presentations.label: HIV screening support
presentations.summary: Useful when HIV testing or HIV status screening is the main concern.

tests.name: HIV ELISA
tests.reason: WebMD HIV testing guidance describes blood-based HIV screening, which matches the current HIV ELISA card.

presentations.caution: Window periods, consent, and post-exposure pathways still need local protocol.

presentations.label: Anaemia / iron deficiency support
presentations.summary: Useful when fatigue, pallor, or low haemoglobin is suspected.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: Baseline blood count support for anaemia workup.

tests.name: Fe Studies
tests.reason: Iron-study profile already available in the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Interpret with bleeding history, pregnancy status, and chronic disease.

presentations.label: Bleeding / bruising support
presentations.summary: Useful when abnormal bleeding or bruising suggests a count or clotting problem.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: Count support for anaemia or thrombocytopenia context.

tests.name: Coagulation Studies
tests.reason: Clotting screen from the existing catalogue.

presentations.caution: Major bleeding is an emergency and needs immediate escalation.

presentations.label: DVT / clot support
presentations.summary: Useful when deep-vein thrombosis or venous thromboembolism is being considered.

tests.name: XDP (D-Dimer)
tests.reason: WebMD DVT diagnosis guidance highlights D-dimer as a blood test used when clot is being considered.

presentations.caution: High-risk DVT or PE still needs urgent imaging and the local clot pathway.

presentations.label: Kidney / proteinuria support
presentations.summary: Useful when oedema, frothy urine, or kidney disease is suspected.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: Baseline renal chemistry support.

tests.name: Albumin:Creatinine Ratio (Random Urine)
tests.reason: Random urine albumin assessment already in the catalogue.

tests.name: Protein:Creatinine Ratio (Random Urine)
tests.reason: Random urine protein quantification from the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Use local nephrology and hypertension pathways for AKI, nephrotic syndrome, or severe oedema.

presentations.label: Hypertension support
presentations.summary: Useful when high blood pressure or hypertension is the main concern.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: WebMD high-blood-pressure testing guidance includes blood tests for electrolytes, urea, and creatinine.

presentations.caution: Severely elevated blood pressure, end-organ symptoms, or pregnancy hypertension still need urgent clinical assessment.

presentations.label: Hyperglycaemia / diabetes support
presentations.summary: Useful when symptomatic hyperglycaemia or diabetes is part of the question.

tests.name: Random Glucose
tests.reason: Immediate glucose support from the current catalogue.

tests.name: HbA1c
tests.reason: Longer-term glycaemic support for diabetes assessment.

presentations.caution: If DKA or HHS is possible, use emergency metabolic pathways instead of this tool alone.

presentations.label: Pregnancy glucose screening support
presentations.summary: Useful when pregnancy glucose screening or gestational diabetes is the concern.

tests.name: OGTT Pregnancy (fasting, 1hr, 2hr)
tests.reason: Dedicated pregnancy OGTT already present in the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Timing and eligibility for antenatal OGTT must follow local antenatal protocol.

presentations.label: Thyroid dysfunction support
presentations.summary: Useful when thyroid disease, goitre, or temperature intolerance is suspected.

tests.name: Thyroid Function Test (TFT)
tests.reason: Core thyroid profile from the current catalogue.

presentations.caution: Interpret with pregnancy status, medication history, and local endocrine pathways.

presentations.label: Jaundice / liver injury support
presentations.summary: Useful when jaundice or hepatitis is suspected, but in infants it should only surface when there are clearer liver or hepatitis clues.

tests.name: Liver Function Tests (LFT)
tests.reason: Liver profile already listed in the catalogue.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: Baseline blood count support for infection, anaemia, and thrombocytopenia context.

tests.name: Coagulation Studies
tests.reason: Clotting support when liver synthetic dysfunction or bleeding risk is a concern.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: Renal and electrolyte support for broader liver disease and decompensation context.

presentations.caution: Severe jaundice, confusion, or coagulopathy needs urgent escalation.

presentations.label: Newborn / infant jaundice bilirubin support
presentations.summary: Useful when newborn or infant jaundice monitoring or bilirubin follow-up is the main concern.

tests.name: Total Serum Bilirubin (TSB)
tests.reason: Preferred bilirubin profile for newborn and infant jaundice monitoring, with total bilirubin and conjugated/direct bilirubin; indirect/unconjugated bilirubin is calculated.

presentations.caution: In newborns, interpret by age in hours and follow the local neonatal jaundice pathway. In older infants, use local prolonged-jaundice and cholestasis guidance.

presentations.label: Acute hepatitis B support
presentations.summary: Useful when acute viral hepatitis B exposure or illness is part of the story.

tests.name: Hepatitis B (Acute)
tests.reason: Acute hepatitis B profile from the existing catalogue.

presentations.caution: Use local infectious-disease or occupational-exposure guidance where relevant.

presentations.label: Pancreatitis support
presentations.summary: Useful when epigastric pain or pain radiating to the back suggests pancreatitis.

tests.name: Lipase
tests.reason: Pancreatic enzyme support from the current catalogue.

tests.name: Amylase
tests.reason: Additional pancreatic enzyme already available in the catalogue.

tests.name: Liver Function Tests (LFT)
tests.reason: Helpful for biliary obstruction or gallstone-related pancreatitis context.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: Baseline blood count support for inflammatory illness severity and complications.

tests.name: CRP
tests.reason: Inflammatory marker support when tracking acute pancreatitis severity.

presentations.caution: Abdominal emergencies still need direct clinical review and imaging decisions; escalate beyond this set if sepsis or infected necrosis is suspected.

presentations.label: Crohn's / ulcerative colitis support
presentations.summary: Useful when inflammatory bowel disease such as Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis is being considered.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: WebMD IBD diagnosis guidance includes blood tests for anaemia and infection support.

tests.name: CRP
tests.reason: WebMD Crohn's and ulcerative-colitis diagnosis pages both describe CRP as an inflammation marker.

tests.name: ESR
tests.reason: WebMD Crohn's diagnosis guidance includes ESR as part of inflammation assessment.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: WebMD Crohn's diagnosis guidance includes an electrolyte panel when diarrhoea or dehydration is part of the picture.

tests.name: Calprotectin (Fecal)
tests.reason: Added as a stool inflammation marker that fits the inflammatory bowel disease workup already carried in the catalogue.

presentations.caution: IBD diagnosis still depends on stool studies, imaging, and endoscopy through the local GI pathway.

presentations.label: STI support
presentations.summary: Useful when STI is the concern.

tests.name: Urine MCS
tests.reason: Urine MC&S from the current catalogue.

presentations.caution: Follow local sexual-health and microbiology collection protocol for the requested specimen.

presentations.label: Female infertility support
presentations.summary: Useful when infertility or ovulatory dysfunction is being assessed in a female context.

tests.name: Infertility Screen (Female)
tests.reason: Female reproductive hormone profile from the catalogue.

tests.name: Thyroid Function Test (TFT)
tests.reason: Thyroid support often paired with infertility assessment.

presentations.caution: Cycle timing and local fertility pathways still apply.

presentations.label: Male infertility support
presentations.summary: Useful when infertility is being assessed in a male context.

tests.name: Infertility Screen (Male)
tests.reason: Male fertility hormone profile from the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Local fertility pathways may also require semen analysis and specialist review.

presentations.label: Hirsutism / androgen excess support
presentations.summary: Useful when androgen excess, acne, or possible PCOS is being assessed.

tests.name: Hirsutism Screen (Full)
tests.reason: Androgen-focused endocrine profile from the catalogue.

tests.name: Thyroid Function Test (TFT)
tests.reason: Thyroid support often complements endocrine workup.

presentations.caution: Interpret with age, menstrual history, and pregnancy context.

presentations.label: Menopausal hormone support
presentations.summary: Useful when menopausal symptoms or hot flushes are part of the concern.

tests.name: Menopausal Screen
tests.reason: Focused menopausal hormone screen from the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Use medication history and local menopause guidance when interpreting results.

presentations.label: Inflammatory joint pain support
presentations.summary: Useful when joint pain, swelling, or morning stiffness suggests inflammatory arthritis.

tests.name: Arthritis Profile
tests.reason: Focused inflammatory arthritis screen already in the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Use broader rheumatology assessment if systemic features are present.

presentations.label: Lupus / SLE support
presentations.summary: Useful when lupus or systemic lupus erythematosus is part of the differential.

tests.name: FBC
tests.reason: WebMD lupus lab guidance includes CBC support when cell lines may be affected.

tests.name: ANA Screen and Reflex ENA Antibodies
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance highlights ANA testing as a key laboratory entry point.

tests.name: Anti-dsDNA
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance specifically lists anti-dsDNA among the core blood tests.

tests.name: Complement C3
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance includes complement testing when lupus activity is being assessed.

tests.name: Complement C4
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance includes complement testing when lupus activity is being assessed.

tests.name: U&E
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance includes kidney-function blood tests such as urea and creatinine.

tests.name: Protein:Creatinine Ratio (Random Urine)
tests.reason: WebMD lupus guidance includes urine testing for renal involvement and protein loss.

presentations.caution: Lupus workup still needs pattern recognition, renal review, and specialist guidance where disease is systemic or active.

presentations.label: Systemic autoimmune support
presentations.summary: Useful when vasculitis, CTD, or systemic autoimmune disease is suspected.

tests.name: Autoimmune Profile
tests.reason: Broader autoimmune screen from the current catalogue.

presentations.caution: Autoimmune workup should still be guided by clinical pattern and specialist advice.

presentations.label: Pregnancy hormone support
presentations.summary: Useful when pregnancy context is selected and serum BHCG support may be needed.

tests.name: BHCG (Beta-HCG)
tests.reason: Serum beta-HCG supports pregnancy confirmation or follow-up when pregnancy context is selected.

presentations.caution: Use the local obstetric, ectopic pregnancy, and ultrasound pathway when bleeding, pain, or instability is present.

presentations.label: Pregnancy booking support
presentations.summary: Useful when antenatal booking or first pregnancy bloods are required, usually at the first antenatal visit.

tests.name: Antenatal Screen (ANTINV)
tests.reason: Use for first antenatal booking bloods, ideally early in pregnancy at the first booking visit.

presentations.caution: Usually done at the first antenatal booking visit as early as possible in pregnancy, ideally in the first trimester. If the patient presents later, local protocol still treats it as the first-visit bundle.

presentations.label: Newborn cord blood support
presentations.summary: Useful in neonatal or delivery context where cord blood testing is requested.

tests.name: Cord Blood
tests.reason: Cord blood profile already listed in the catalogue.

presentations.caution: Use the local neonatal and obstetric pathway for identification and timing steps.

presentations.label: Hepatic encephalopathy support
presentations.summary: Useful when confusion occurs together with features of liver failure or cirrhosis.

tests.name: Ammonia
tests.reason: Time-sensitive ammonia handling support from the current catalogue.

presentations.caution: This sample is highly time-sensitive and must follow the critical handling note on the test card.
